breakdown: This PR widens state flag boolean semantics in game_state.py to accept numeric LLM outputs (int 1/0, string '1'/'0') in addition to existing True/False and string 'true'/'false'. The change fixes silent level-up stalls caused by LLMs setting boolean fields as integers. Tests provide thorough coverage of accepted/rejected values including the critical bool-subclass-of-int edge case. Code is well-documented with docstrings explaining the production problem being solved.
key_changes: Widen _is_state_flag_true to accept int 1 and string '1' in addition to True/'true'
Widen _is_state_flag_false to accept int 0 and string '0' in addition to False/'false'
Explicitly exclude bool from int check to prevent bool(1) == True confusing the int branch
Add comprehensive StateFlagSemanticsTest with test_true_accepted_values, test_true_rejected_values, test_false_accepted_values, test_false_rejected_values, and test_bool_subclass_of_int_not_confused
